seoTitle: How to Integrate Jira Software With GitHub
metaDescription: Connect Jira Software with GitHub so commits, branches, and pull requests automatically link back to the right issues.
intro: Switching between Jira and GitHub to track which code change belongs to which issue wastes time. Integrating the two lets commits, branches, and pull requests show up directly on the related Jira issue. Here's how to connect them.

steps.heading: Open the Jira integrations settings
steps.body: Go to your Jira instance's app or integrations settings where development tool connections are managed.

steps.heading: Install the GitHub for Jira app
steps.body: Add the official GitHub integration from the marketplace and start the connection process.

steps.heading: Authorize your GitHub account
steps.body: Sign in to GitHub and grant Jira access to the organization and repositories you want linked.

steps.heading: Select repositories to connect
steps.body: Choose which repositories should sync with Jira so their activity shows up on relevant issues.

steps.heading: Reference issue keys in commits
steps.body: Include the Jira issue key in commit messages or branch names so activity automatically links to the correct issue.

faq.question: How does GitHub know which Jira issue to link to?
faq.answer: It matches the Jira issue key included in your commit message, branch name, or pull request title.

faq.question: Does this integration cost extra?
faq.answer: The GitHub for Jira app itself is free to install, though your Jira and GitHub plans still apply separately.

faq.question: Can I see pull request status directly on a Jira issue?
faq.answer: Yes, once connected, the development panel on the issue shows linked branches, commits, and pull request status.

faq.question: Will this work with GitHub Enterprise?
faq.answer: GitHub for Jira supports GitHub Enterprise Server in addition to GitHub.com, though setup steps can differ slightly.
